description_long: This object is a vintage-style stapler with a rectangular wooden base and metallic components. The upper arm is a long, slender metal lever with a rounded, flat press handle at one end and a circular knob at the other, connected by a visible hinge mechanism. The metallic parts have a dark, aged patina, suggesting brass or bronze construction, and the base shows wood grain and some wear. The staple exit slot is located at the front end of the base, beneath the pressing arm, and is surrounded by a metal plate. There are no visible brand logos or markings on the surface. The overall design is utilitarian, with exposed screws and a robust, industrial appearance.
description: A vintage stapler with a wooden base and dark metal lever arm, featuring exposed mechanical parts and a robust, industrial look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular press handle and the staple exit slot at the end of the wooden base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long metal lever arm, hinge mechanism, and the full length of the wooden base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the press handle and the alignment of the lever arm over the base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the lever arm, hinge, and the side profile of the wooden base.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is a circular, flat metal disc with a slightly convex top, matching the dark bronze color of the lever. The surface appears smooth with no visible ergonomic patterns or texturing.
attribute_metal_hinge_mechanism: The metal hinge mechanism is located near the center of the base, connecting the lever arm to the wooden platform. It consists of a cylindrical pin and visible fasteners, allowing the lever to pivot smoothly.
attribute_staple_exit_slot: The staple exit slot is positioned at the front end of the base, directly beneath the pressing handle. It is a small rectangular opening surrounded by a metal plate, clearly visible in the front view.
